2604.10853.title: A Benchmark for Gap and Overlap Analysis as a Test of KG Task Readiness
2604.10853.paper_summary: Task-oriented evaluation of knowledge graph (KG) quality increasingly asks whether an ontology-based representation can answer the competency questions that users actually care about, in a manner that is reproducible, explainable, and traceable to evidence. This paper adopts that perspective and focuses on gap and overlap analysis for policy-like documents (e.g., insurance contracts), where given a scenario, which documents support it (overlap) and which do not (gap), with defensible justifications. The resulting gap/overlap determinations are typically driven by genuine differences in coverage and restrictions rather than missing data, making the task a direct test of KG task readiness rather than a test of missing facts or query expressiveness. We present an executable and auditable benchmark that aligns natural-language contract text with a formal ontology and evidence-linked ground truth, enabling systematic comparison of methods. The benchmark includes: (i) ten simplified yet diverse life-insurance contracts reviewed by a domain expert, (ii) a domain ontology (TBox) with an instantiated knowledge base (ABox) populated from contract facts, and (iii) 58 structured scenarios paired with SPARQL queries with contract-level outcomes and clause-level excerpts that justify each label. Using this resource, we compare a text-only LLM baseline that infers outcomes directly from contract text against an ontology-driven pipeline that answers the same scenarios over the instantiated KG, demonstrating that explicit modeling improves consistency and diagnosis for gap/overlap analyses. Although demonstrated for gap and overlap analysis, the benchmark is intended as a reusable template for evaluating KG quality and supporting downstream work such as ontology learning, KG population, and evidence-grounded question answering.
2604.10853.paper_summary_zh: 任務導向的知識圖譜（KG）質量評估越來越關注基於本體的表示是否能以可重複、可解釋且可追溯到證據的方式回答用戶實際關心的能力問題。本文採取這一觀點，專注於政策類文件（例如保險合同）的差距和重疊分析，在給定場景的情況下，哪些文件支持該場景（重疊），哪些不支持（差距），並提供合理的理由。所產生的差距/重疊判定通常是由於覆蓋範圍和限制的真實差異，而不是數據缺失，這使得該任務成為對KG任務準備狀態的直接測試，而不是對缺失事實或查詢表達能力的測試。我們提出了一個可執行且可審計的基準，將自然語言合同文本與正式本體和證據鏈接的真實情況對齊，從而實現方法的系統比較。該基準包括：（i）十份經過領域專家審查的簡化但多樣的壽險合同，（ii）一個領域本體（TBox）及其從合同事實填充的實例化知識庫（ABox），以及（iii）58個結構化場景，配對SPARQL查詢，包含合同級結果和逐條摘錄以證明每個標籤的合理性。利用這一資源，我們比較了一個僅基於文本的LLM基準，該基準直接從合同文本推斷結果，與一個基於本體的管道，該管道在實例化的KG上回答相同的場景，證明明確建模提高了差距/重疊分析的一致性和診斷能力。雖然已經針對差距和重疊分析進行了演示，但該基準旨在作為評估KG質量和支持下游工作（如本體學習、KG填充和基於證據的問題回答）的可重用模板。
2604.10853.author: Maruf Ahmed Mridul et.al.
2604.10853.authors: Maruf Ahmed Mridul, Rohit Kapa, Oshani Seneviratne
